assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 2 Trace 1 identifies 'The formatter applies a slugify/kebab-case transformation to the heading text (e.g., "Migration Patterns" becomes "migration-patterns")'. Step 2 Trace 2 identifies 'The logic reads Feature issue type ID (10142) from the Jira Configuration section instead of using the correct Task issue type ID (10050)'. Step 4 explicitly labels these as 'Root Cause 1 -- Malformed convention references' in shared/convention-utils.md and 'Root Cause 2 -- Wrong issue type on task creation' in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a, with a summary table stating 'The two root causes are independent'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md states: 'This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6) -- a single Task bundling both fixes would be inappropriate.' decomposition-guard.md header reads '## Triggered: Multiple Independent Root Causes Detected' and states 'These two issues are in independent code paths in different modules. They do not share logic, do not interact, and fixing one has no effect on the other.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents both issues with files: (1) 'Malformed convention references (in shared/convention-utils.md) -- The convention reference formatter applies a kebab-case/slugify transformation...' and (2) 'Wrong issue type on task creation (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a) -- The task creation logic reads the Feature issue type ID (10142)...'. A summary table also lists 'Bug A | Convention reference formatter produces kebab-case instead of preserving heading text | shared/convention-utils.md' and 'Bug B | plan-feature Step 6a creates tasks with Feature issue type instead of Task issue type | plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md explicitly lists: 'Options: 1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes 2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually' followed by 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with 'Choose (1/2):' -- an explicit prompt waiting for user input. No task creation output files exist in the outputs directory (only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md). The investigation.md concludes at root cause analysis and states 'This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6)' without proceeding to task creation. No Jira create_issue calls or task artifacts appear anywhere in the outputs.
